nodes.label: Feature: Get Active Subscription
nodes.description: Specification for retrieving the current active subscription for a member via GET /api/members/active-subscription

nodes.label: Feature: Get Member Detail
nodes.description: Specification for retrieving comprehensive member info including personal data, active subscription, health profile, and NFC card

nodes.label: Feature: Get Subscription History
nodes.description: Specification for retrieving full subscription history for a member with sorting and resource-based authorization

nodes.label: Feature: List Members
nodes.description: Specification for paginated search endpoint for admins/coaches with keyword and status filtering

nodes.label: Feature: Manage Subscription Plans
nodes.description: Specification for full CRUD management of gym subscription plans restricted to Admin role

nodes.label: Feature: Update Member
nodes.description: Specification for self-update and administrative update endpoints with Identity Service sync and file uploads

nodes.label: Membership Service Instructions
nodes.description: Master guidelines for Membership service covering architecture, folder structure, implementation patterns, and key conventions

nodes.label: Aspire CLI Skill
nodes.description: Skill definition for orchestrating Aspire distributed applications via CLI commands

nodes.label: Playwright CLI Skill
nodes.description: Skill definition for browser automation, web page testing, and Playwright test workflows

nodes.label: Playwright Element Attributes Reference
nodes.description: Reference for inspecting DOM element attributes (id, class, data-* attributes) via playwright-cli eval

nodes.label: Playwright Tests Reference
nodes.description: Reference for running and debugging Playwright tests with --debug=cli and playwright-cli attachment

nodes.label: Member
nodes.description: Gym member aggregate root with personal details, status, and linked health profile and NFC card

nodes.label: Subscription
nodes.description: Member subscription to a plan with start/end dates, status, and plan reference

nodes.label: Subscription Plan
nodes.description: Gym subscription plan with name, price, duration, and IsActive soft-delete flag

nodes.label: Health Profile
nodes.description: Member health information including objectives and medical restrictions

nodes.label: NFC Card
nodes.description: Physical NFC card linked to a member for gym access entry/exit logging

nodes.label: Get Active Subscription
nodes.description: Retrieve current active subscription for a member with date-range and status validation

nodes.label: Get Member Detail
nodes.description: Retrieve comprehensive member information including active subscription, health profile, and NFC card

nodes.label: Get Subscription History
nodes.description: Retrieve full sorted history of all subscriptions (past, current, future) for a member

nodes.label: List Members
nodes.description: Paginated search and filter of gym members for administrators and coaches

nodes.label: Manage Subscription Plans
nodes.description: Admin-only CRUD management of subscription plans including soft-delete

nodes.label: Update Member
nodes.description: Self-update and administrative update of member details with Identity Service sync and file uploads

nodes.label: Create Member
nodes.description: Create new gym member with Identity Service integration, file uploads, and MemberCreatedEvent publishing

nodes.label: Delete/Deactivate Member
nodes.description: Soft-delete member with Identity Service deactivation

nodes.label: Health Profile Management
nodes.description: Get and update member health profile data (goals, medical restrictions)

nodes.label: NFC Card Management
nodes.description: Assign/replace NFC cards, lifecycle management, and entry/exit event logging

nodes.label: GET /api/members/active-subscription
nodes.description: Retrieve current active subscription for authenticated member

nodes.label: GET /api/members/{id}
nodes.description: Retrieve comprehensive member details by ID

nodes.label: GET /api/members/{id}/subscriptions
nodes.description: Retrieve subscription history for a specific member

nodes.label: GET /api/members
nodes.description: Paginated list of members with search and status filtering

nodes.label: POST /api/plans
nodes.description: Create a new subscription plan (Admin only)

nodes.label: GET /api/plans
nodes.description: List all active subscription plans

nodes.label: GET /api/plans/{id}
nodes.description: Get subscription plan details by ID

nodes.label: PUT /api/plans/{id}
nodes.description: Update subscription plan details (Admin only)

nodes.label: DELETE /api/plans/{id}
nodes.description: Soft-delete a subscription plan (Admin only)

nodes.label: PUT /api/members/my-profile
nodes.description: Self-update of member personal details, profile picture, date of birth, health profile, and password

nodes.label: PUT /api/members/{id}
nodes.description: Administrative update of any member's personal details, status, and health profile

nodes.label: ASP.NET Core
nodes.description: Web framework for building API endpoints and middleware

nodes.label: Entity Framework Core
nodes.description: ORM for data access with projections, AsNoTracking, and migrations

nodes.label: Wolverine
nodes.description: Message mediation library used for handler invocation via IMessageBus and event publishing

nodes.label: Carter
nodes.description: Lightweight library for building Minimal API endpoints with ICarterModule

nodes.label: Refit
nodes.description: HTTP client library for synchronous inter-service communication via typed interfaces

nodes.label: FluentValidation
nodes.description: Validation library used as Wolverine middleware via ValidationBehavior

nodes.label: ErrorOr
nodes.description: Result pattern library returning ErrorOr<T> from handlers for standardized error mapping

nodes.label: RabbitMQ
nodes.description: Message broker for asynchronous cross-service communication via Wolverine/MassTransit

nodes.label: MassTransit
nodes.description: Message bus library used for async events (Notification, Identity services)

nodes.label: Aspire
nodes.description: Distributed application orchestrator with CLI for running, debugging, and managing microservices

nodes.label: Playwright CLI
nodes.description: Browser automation CLI tool for functional testing, web scraping, and UI validation

nodes.label: YARP
nodes.description: Reverse proxy gateway routing requests to backend services

nodes.label: Vertical Slice Architecture
nodes.description: Architecture pattern organizing code by feature rather than layer, with per-slice endpoint/handler/request/response/validator files

nodes.label: Role-Based Access Control
nodes.description: Authorization model with Admin, Coach, and Member roles enforced via UserAccessor

nodes.label: OpenAPI Documentation
nodes.description: API documentation using built-in OpenAPI with transformers, tags, and XML descriptions and examples

nodes.label: Soft Delete Pattern
nodes.description: Data integrity pattern using IsActive flag instead of physical deletion

nodes.label: Cross-Service Events
nodes.description: Shared integration events published via Wolverine/MassTransit to RabbitMQ for asynchronous inter-service communication

nodes.label: Pagination Pattern
nodes.description: Skip/Take pagination with keyword search and status filtering for listing endpoints

nodes.label: Identity Service Integration
nodes.description: Synchronous Refit HTTP client calls to Identity service for user profile updates, password changes, and file uploads

nodes.label: EF Core Projections
nodes.description: Direct Select() projections with AsNoTracking to avoid over-fetching and unnecessary entity tracking

nodes.label: ErrorOr Result Pattern
nodes.description: Standardized error handling via ErrorOr<T> with MapErrorsToResult mapping NotFound, Conflict, Validation errors to HTTP status codes

nodes.label: Validation Behavior
nodes.description: Wolverine middleware pipeline integrating FluentValidation for automatic request validation

nodes.label: UserAccessor Pattern
nodes.description: Security utility in Common/Security that retrieves current UserId and roles for authorization decisions

nodes.label: Resource-Based Authorization
nodes.description: Authorization pattern checking if the requesting user is the resource owner (self) or has admin/coach role

nodes.label: MembershipDbContext
nodes.description: EF Core DbContext for the Membership service database

nodes.label: Admin Role
nodes.description: Administrator role with full access to all endpoints including plan management

nodes.label: Coach Role
nodes.description: Coach role with access to member listing and detail endpoints

nodes.label: Member Role
nodes.description: Standard member role with self-service access to own profile and subscription data

nodes.label: Shared.Events Namespace
nodes.description: Namespace containing shared integration events for cross-service communication

nodes.label: IIdentityServiceClient
nodes.description: Refit HTTP client interface for synchronous Identity service calls including UpdateProfileAsync and ChangePasswordAsync

nodes.label: Aspire CLI Commands
nodes.description: CLI commands for orchestration: start, stop, describe, logs, otel, wait, resource management, docs, add, mcp

nodes.label: Playwright CLI Commands
nodes.description: CLI commands for browser automation: open, goto, click, fill, snapshot, eval, tracing, video, storage, network, devtools

nodes.label: Request Mocking
nodes.description: Intercept, mock, modify, and block network requests using Playwright routing API

nodes.label: URL Patterns
nodes.description: Glob-style URL matching patterns for request routing: exact path, wildcard, file extension, and query parameter matching

nodes.label: Conditional Response Mocking
nodes.description: Mock responses based on request body inspection, returning different responses for different inputs

nodes.label: Real Response Modification
nodes.description: Intercept real network responses, modify the response body/headers, and fulfill with modified data

nodes.label: Network Failure Simulation
nodes.description: Simulate network errors like connection refused, timeout, reset, and internet disconnected

nodes.label: Delayed Response Mocking
nodes.description: Introduce artificial delays before fulfilling network requests to test loading states and timeouts

nodes.label: Custom Code Execution
nodes.description: Execute arbitrary Playwright JavaScript code for advanced scenarios via run-code command

nodes.label: Geolocation Emulation
nodes.description: Set and clear browser geolocation coordinates for location-based testing

nodes.label: Browser Permissions
nodes.description: Grant and clear browser permissions including geolocation, notifications, camera, microphone, and clipboard

nodes.label: Media Emulation
nodes.description: Emulate color scheme (dark/light), reduced motion, and print media for responsive design testing

nodes.label: Wait Strategies
nodes.description: Wait mechanisms for network idle, element visibility, custom conditions, and timeout-based waits

nodes.label: Frame and Iframe Handling
nodes.description: Interact with iframes by obtaining content frames, clicking elements, and enumerating all frames

nodes.label: File Download Handling
nodes.description: Capture file downloads triggered by clicks, save files to disk, and retrieve suggested filenames

nodes.label: Clipboard Operations
nodes.description: Read from and write to the browser clipboard with appropriate permissions

nodes.label: JavaScript Execution
nodes.description: Execute arbitrary JavaScript in the page context and retrieve return values including user agent, language, cookies

nodes.label: Browser Session Management
nodes.description: Run multiple isolated browser sessions concurrently with independent cookies, storage, cache, history, and tabs

nodes.label: Named Browser Sessions
nodes.description: Isolate browser contexts by name using -s flag for parallel workflows with separate state

nodes.label: Concurrent Scraping
nodes.description: Run multiple browser sessions in parallel to scrape data from multiple sites simultaneously

nodes.label: A/B Testing Sessions
nodes.description: Compare different page variants simultaneously using separate browser sessions for side-by-side differences

nodes.label: Persistent Browser Profiles
nodes.description: Persist browser profiles to disk using --persistent flag for state preservation across sessions

nodes.label: Browser Attachment
nodes.description: Attach to already-running browsers via CDP channel name, endpoint URL, or browser extension

nodes.label: CDP Attachment
nodes.description: Connect to browsers via Chrome DevTools Protocol by channel name (chrome, msedge) or endpoint URL

nodes.label: Spec-Driven Testing
nodes.description: End-to-end workflow for authoring and maintaining Playwright tests: Plan -> Generate -> Heal

nodes.label: Spec File
nodes.description: Markdown file describing feature test plan with application overview, scenarios, steps, and expected outcomes

nodes.label: Seed Test
nodes.description: Minimal Playwright test that navigates to the starting page, optionally via a shared fixture

nodes.label: Test Fixture
nodes.description: Shared Playwright test fixture that encapsulates common setup like page navigation for reuse across tests

nodes.label: Test Healing
nodes.description: Diagnose and fix failing tests: update locators, fix assertions, reconcile spec with changed app behavior

nodes.label: Selector Drift
nodes.description: Test failure caused by UI changes where element selectors, labels, or ARIA roles no longer match the page

nodes.label: Storage State Management
nodes.description: Save and restore complete browser state including cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage for state persistence

nodes.label: Cookie Management
nodes.description: List, filter, get, set, delete, and clear browser cookies with domain, path, secure, httpOnly options

nodes.label: LocalStorage Management
nodes.description: Manage localStorage key-value pairs including JSON values, single operations, and bulk operations

nodes.label: SessionStorage Management
nodes.description: Manage sessionStorage key-value pairs for temporary session-scoped data

nodes.label: IndexedDB Management
nodes.description: List databases and delete IndexedDB databases via custom code execution

nodes.label: Authentication State Reuse
nodes.description: Save authenticated browser state after login and restore it later to skip authentication steps

nodes.label: Test Generation
nodes.description: Automatic generation of Playwright TypeScript test code from interactive CLI actions

nodes.label: Semantic Locators
nodes.description: Role-based and accessible name-based element selectors that are more resilient than CSS selectors

nodes.label: Playwright Assertions
nodes.description: Built-in assertion matchers: toBeVisible, toHaveText, toHaveValue, toBeChecked, toMatchAriaSnapshot

nodes.label: Tracing
nodes.description: Capture detailed execution traces with DOM snapshots, screenshots, network activity, and console logs

nodes.label: Trace Files
nodes.description: Trace output files: action log (.trace), network log (.network), and cached resources directory

nodes.label: Performance Analysis
nodes.description: Analyze execution performance via traces including network waterfalls, timing, and resource sizes

nodes.label: Video Recording
nodes.description: Capture browser automation as WebM video with chapter markers and overlay annotations

nodes.label: Screencast API
nodes.description: Playwright screencast API for video recording with showChapter, showOverlay, hideOverlays, and dispose methods

nodes.label: Video Chapter Markers
nodes.description: Full-screen chapter cards with blurred backdrop for section transitions in video recordings

nodes.label: Overlay Annotations
nodes.description: Custom HTML overlays for callouts, labels, highlights with pointer-events:none for non-interference

nodes.label: Trace vs Video Comparison
nodes.description: Comparison of tracing and video recording: tracing for debugging (DOM, network, console) vs video for demos

nodes.label: Graphify
nodes.description: Knowledge graph query tool for codebase navigation with query, path, explain commands and graph-out/ output

nodes.label: C# 14
nodes.description: Latest C# language version with primary constructors, record types, collection expressions, field keyword, and extension members

nodes.label: C# Naming Conventions
nodes.description: PascalCase for components/methods/public members, camelCase for private fields, I-prefix for interfaces

nodes.label: File-Scoped Namespaces
nodes.description: C# namespace declarations without braces, reducing indentation and improving readability

nodes.label: Nullable Reference Types
nodes.description: C# 8+ feature for null-state analysis with is null/is not null pattern instead of == null

nodes.label: JWT Authentication
nodes.description: JSON Web Token bearer token authentication for ASP.NET Core APIs with role and policy-based authorization

nodes.label: OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect
nodes.description: Authorization framework and identity layer for ASP.NET Core with Microsoft Entra ID integration

nodes.label: FluentValidation
nodes.description: Validation library for .NET with pipeline customization, used in conjunction with ProblemDetails error responses

nodes.label: ProblemDetails (RFC 9457)
nodes.description: Standardized error response format for HTTP APIs with global exception handling middleware

nodes.label: Serilog Structured Logging
nodes.description: Structured logging provider with sinks, enrichers, and Application Insights integration for .NET

nodes.label: API Versioning
nodes.description: URL segment, header, and query string versioning strategies with deprecation and OpenAPI integration

nodes.label: Caching Strategies
nodes.description: In-memory, distributed, and response caching patterns for API performance optimization

nodes.label: Asynchronous Programming
nodes.description: Async/await patterns for API performance including proper task handling and avoiding sync-over-async

nodes.label: Container Publishing
nodes.description: NET built-in container support via dotnet publish without Dockerfile, with health checks and readiness probes

nodes.label: CI/CD Pipelines
nodes.description: Continuous integration and deployment pipelines for .NET applications with environment-specific config

nodes.label: Aspire AppHost
nodes.description: Central project defining resource graph, integrations, and service orchestration for distributed applications

nodes.label: Aspire Resource Management
nodes.description: Manage individual resources: start, stop, restart, rebuild, check status via aspire CLI

nodes.label: Aspire Integrations
nodes.description: NuGet packages adding service dependencies (databases, message brokers) to Aspire AppHost

nodes.label: Aspire Distributed Tracing
nodes.description: Distributed trace and structured log viewing across resources via aspire otel CLI commands

nodes.label: Aspire MCP Tools
nodes.description: Resources exposing MCP tools (e.g. WithPostgresMcp) for database queries via CLI

nodes.label: Aspire Isolated Mode
nodes.description: Run Aspire with --isolated flag in git worktrees to avoid port conflicts and secret sharing

nodes.label: Pattern Matching
nodes.description: C# pattern matching and switch expressions for concise conditional logic

nodes.label: Record Types
nodes.description: C# reference types with value-based equality, immutability, and concise syntax for data-carrying types

nodes.label: Primary Constructors
nodes.description: C# 12+ feature allowing constructor parameters directly on class/struct declaration with captured scope

nodes.label: Git Worktree
nodes.description: Git feature for checking out multiple branches simultaneously, used with Aspire isolated mode

nodes.label: Remote Browser Debugging
nodes.description: Enable remote debugging in Chrome/Edge via chrome://inspect for CDP attachment

nodes.label: Element References
nodes.description: Numeric element references (e1, e2, e3) from CLI snapshot output used for targeting interactive elements

nodes.label: playwright-cli Skill (.github copy)
nodes.description: Duplicate of the playwright-cli skill stored under .github/skills/ for browser automation and Playwright test workflows

nodes.label: Browser Automation
nodes.description: Core browser automation capabilities: open, goto, click, type, fill, snapshot, eval, and navigation commands

nodes.label: playwright-cli open
nodes.description: Open a new browser session, optionally navigating to a URL with browser choice, persistent profile, or CDP attach

nodes.label: playwright-cli goto
nodes.description: Navigate to a URL in the current browser session

nodes.label: playwright-cli snapshot
nodes.description: Take an accessibility snapshot of the current page or a specific element, with optional depth and bounding box settings

nodes.label: playwright-cli click
nodes.description: Click an element by ref, CSS selector, or Playwright locator

nodes.label: playwright-cli fill
nodes.description: Fill a form element with text, supports --submit flag to press Enter after

nodes.label: playwright-cli eval
nodes.description: Execute JavaScript on the page or against a specific element ref

nodes.label: playwright-cli resize
nodes.description: Resize the browser viewport to specified width and height

nodes.label: playwright-cli screenshot
nodes.description: Capture a screenshot of the page or a specific element

nodes.label: playwright-cli pdf
nodes.description: Save the page as a PDF file

nodes.label: playwright-cli drag
nodes.description: Drag and drop from one element to another

nodes.label: playwright-cli drop
nodes.description: Drop files or MIME-typed data onto an element from outside the page

nodes.label: playwright-cli hover
nodes.description: Hover over an element on the page

nodes.label: playwright-cli select
nodes.description: Select an option in a dropdown element

nodes.label: playwright-cli upload
nodes.description: Upload one or more files via the file chooser

nodes.label: playwright-cli check / uncheck
nodes.description: Check or uncheck a checkbox element

nodes.label: playwright-cli dialog-accept / dialog-dismiss
nodes.description: Accept or dismiss browser dialogs, optionally with confirmation text

nodes.label: playwright-cli press / keydown / keyup
nodes.description: Keyboard interaction commands: press keys, hold modifiers

nodes.label: playwright-cli mouse commands
nodes.description: Mouse interaction commands: mousemove, mousedown, mouseup, mousewheel

nodes.label: playwright-cli tab commands
nodes.description: Tab management: list, new, close, select tabs

nodes.label: playwright-cli cookie commands
nodes.description: Cookie management: list, get, set, delete, clear cookies

nodes.label: playwright-cli localstorage commands
nodes.description: LocalStorage management: list, get, set, delete, clear

nodes.label: playwright-cli sessionstorage commands
nodes.description: SessionStorage management: list, get, set, delete, clear

nodes.label: playwright-cli state-save / state-load
nodes.description: Save and restore complete browser storage state including cookies and localStorage

nodes.label: playwright-cli route / unroute
nodes.description: Intercept and mock network requests with custom status, body, headers, or remove headers

nodes.label: playwright-cli console
nodes.description: View browser console messages filtered by level

nodes.label: playwright-cli requests / request
nodes.description: View network requests made by the page

nodes.label: playwright-cli run-code
nodes.description: Execute arbitrary Playwright code as an async function, optionally from a file

nodes.label: playwright-cli tracing-start / tracing-stop
nodes.description: Start and stop capturing execution traces with DOM snapshots, screenshots, and network activity

nodes.label: playwright-cli video commands
nodes.description: Video recording: video-start, video-chapter, video-stop with chapter markers and overlays

nodes.label: playwright-cli generate-locator
nodes.description: Generate a Playwright locator expression from an element ref

nodes.label: playwright-cli highlight
nodes.description: Show or hide persistent highlight overlay for an element

nodes.description: Launch the annotation dashboard for UI review — user annotates the page and you receive the result

nodes.label: playwright-cli attach
nodes.description: Connect to a running browser via CDP, channel name, or Playwright extension

nodes.label: playwright-cli -s (session flag)
nodes.description: Isolate browser sessions with independent cookies, storage, and tabs using the -s flag

nodes.description: The --raw flag strips status and snapshot sections for piping output into other tools

nodes.description: Interact with elements via snapshot refs, CSS selectors, role locators, or test IDs

nodes.description: Intercept, mock, modify, and block network requests using route/run-code

nodes.description: Execute arbitrary Playwright code for geolocation, permissions, media emulation, waits, frames, downloads, clipboard, and page information

nodes.description: End-to-end workflow: plan specs, generate Playwright tests from specs, heal failing tests with debug/attach

nodes.description: Manage cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, and complete browser storage state save/restore

nodes.description: Capture browser sessions as WebM video with chapter markers, overlays, and screencast API

nodes.description: Inspect element DOM properties using eval when snapshot does not reveal id, class, or data-* attributes

nodes.description: Run and debug Playwright tests via npx playwright test with --debug=cli attach workflow

nodes.description: Conditional responses, modify real responses, simulate network failures, and delayed responses via run-code

nodes.label: Aspire Orchestration Skill (.opencode copy)
nodes.description: Skill for orchestrating distributed .NET applications via Aspire CLI. Covers AppHost management, resource lifecycle, integration packages, MCP tools, observability, and diagnostics.

nodes.label: Aspire CLI
nodes.description: Command-line tool for running, debugging, and managing Aspire distributed applications. Primary interface for developers interacting with the AppHost.

nodes.label: AppHost
nodes.description: Central project defining the resource graph of an Aspire distributed application. Resources include .NET projects, containers, databases, message brokers, etc.

nodes.label: Aspire Resource
nodes.description: A component in the Aspire resource graph (e.g. .NET project, container, database, cache). Each resource can be started, stopped, restarted, and rebuilt individually.

nodes.label: Aspire Integration
nodes.description: NuGet package that adds service capabilities to an AppHost (e.g. PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ). Added via 'aspire add' and documented at aspire.dev.

nodes.label: aspire start
nodes.description: Starts the AppHost and all resources. Automatically stops previous instance. Supports --isolated for worktree environments.

nodes.label: aspire stop
nodes.description: Stops the running AppHost and all its resources.

nodes.label: aspire describe
nodes.description: Lists all resources with their status, endpoints, health, and available commands. Supports --format Json for machine-readable output.

nodes.label: aspire resource <name> <command>
nodes.description: Runs a command on a specific resource: start, stop, restart, rebuild. 'rebuild' rebuilds and restarts only that .NET project resource.

nodes.label: aspire logs
nodes.description: Views console output from resources. Accepts an optional resource name filter.

nodes.label: aspire otel
nodes.description: OpenTelemetry subcommands: 'aspire otel logs [resource]' for structured logs, 'aspire otel traces [resource]' for distributed traces, 'aspire otel logs --trace-id <id>' for trace-scoped logs.

nodes.label: aspire add
nodes.description: Adds an integration NuGet package to the AppHost project interactively.

nodes.label: aspire docs
nodes.description: Subcommands: search (full-text search of aspire.dev), get (retrieve page by slug), list (browse available pages).

nodes.label: aspire doctor
nodes.description: Environment diagnostics tool that validates prerequisites (Docker, .NET SDK, etc.) before running AppHost.

nodes.label: aspire mcp
nodes.description: Lists and calls MCP tools exposed by resources (e.g. WithPostgresMcp for SQL queries). Supports --input for JSON-encoded parameters.

nodes.label: aspire wait
nodes.description: Blocks execution until a specific resource reports as healthy. Used in scripts after 'aspire start'.

nodes.label: Isolated Mode
nodes.description: Flag --isolated for 'aspire start' to avoid port conflicts and state sharing when running in git worktrees.

nodes.label: Resource MCP Tools
nodes.description: Expose domain-specific tooling via MCP protocol (e.g. WithPostgresMcp adds SQL query tools). Discoverable via 'aspire mcp tools'.

nodes.label: Aspire Observability
nodes.description: Structured logging, distributed tracing, and console output for resources via OpenTelemetry. Queried with 'aspire otel' subcommands.

nodes.label: Aspire Documentation
nodes.description: Official documentation at aspire.dev and learn.microsoft.com/microsoft/aspire. Accessed via 'aspire docs search/get/list' CLI commands.

nodes.label: Aspire Debug Workflow
nodes.description: Systematic debugging process: 1) aspire describe for status, 2) aspire otel logs for structured logs, 3) aspire logs for console output, 4) aspire otel traces for distributed traces.

nodes.label: Aspire Code Change Workflow
nodes.description: Full restart (aspire start) for AppHost changes; per-resource rebuild (aspire resource <name> rebuild) for .NET project changes. Interpreted resources may auto-reload.

nodes.label: Playwright CLI Skill (.opencode copy)
nodes.description: Skill for browser automation, web testing, and test generation using the playwright-cli tool. Covers core commands, sessions, snapshots, mocking, tracing, and spec-driven testing.

nodes.label: playwright-cli
nodes.description: CLI tool for browser automation. Supports navigation, interaction, snapshots, storage management, request mocking, tracing, video recording, and test generation via TypeScript code output.

nodes.label: Core Browser Commands
nodes.description: open, goto, click, dblclick, type, fill, drag, drop, hover, select, upload, check, uncheck, snapshot, eval, dialog-accept/dismiss, resize, close.

nodes.label: Accessibility Snapshot
nodes.description: DOM accessibility tree snapshot with element references (e.g. e1, e2). Supports depth limiting, element-scoping, and bounding box inclusion. Default interaction target for playwright-cli.

nodes.label: Element References
nodes.description: Snapshot-generated element identifiers (e.g. e15) used for interaction. Also supports CSS selectors, Playwright locators (getByRole, getByTestId), and direct eval for attribute inspection.

nodes.label: Browser Sessions
nodes.description: Named, isolated browser contexts with independent cookies, storage, cache, history, and tabs. Managed via -s flag, list/close/close-all/kill-all commands, and persistent profiles.

nodes.label: Storage State Management
nodes.description: Save/restore cookies, localStorage, and sessionStorage to/from JSON files. Supports individual cookie CRUD, localStorage CRUD, sessionStorage CRUD, and IndexedDB inspection.

nodes.label: Request Mocking
nodes.description: Intercept and mock network requests via route/unroute commands. Supports custom status, JSON body, headers, URL patterns (glob), and advanced scenarios via run-code.

nodes.label: Execution Tracing
nodes.description: Start/stop trace recording capturing actions, DOM snapshots, screenshots, network activity, console logs, and timing. Outputs .trace, .network, and resources/ files.

nodes.label: Test Generation
nodes.description: Every playwright-cli action generates corresponding Playwright TypeScript code. Code can be copied into test files. Supports semantic locators (role-based) and manual assertion addition.

nodes.label: Spec-Driven Testing
nodes.description: End-to-end workflow: plan (explore + spec file), generate (walk spec steps with CLI → emit test code), heal (debug failures, fix locators, reconcile spec). Uses seed tests and debug mode.

nodes.label: Playwright Test Runner
nodes.description: npx playwright test command for running tests. Supports --debug=cli for interactive debugging with playwright-cli attach. PLAYWRIGHT_HTML_OPEN=never suppresses HTML report.

nodes.label: Debug Mode (--debug=cli)
nodes.description: Pauses test at start and prints debugging instructions with a session name (tw-XXXX). playwright-cli attach tw-XXXX connects the CLI to the paused test page for interactive exploration.

nodes.label: Custom Code Execution (run-code)
nodes.description: Executes arbitrary Playwright JavaScript for advanced scenarios: geolocation, permissions, media emulation, wait strategies, iframes, file downloads, clipboard, page info, error handling.

nodes.label: Video Recording
nodes.description: Start/stop video capture (.webm) with chapter markers, descriptions, and duration controls. Referenced in SKILL.md but separate reference file.

nodes.label: Element Attributes Reference
nodes.description: Guide for inspecting element id, class, data-* attributes, computed styles, and DOM properties using eval() when snapshot does not reveal them.

nodes.label: Playwright Tests Reference
nodes.description: Guide for running Playwright tests with npx playwright test and debugging with --debug=cli mode using playwright-cli attach for interactive sessions.

nodes.label: Request Mocking Reference
nodes.description: Advanced request mocking patterns: conditional responses, real response modification, network failure simulation, delayed responses, URL glob patterns.

nodes.label: Running Code Reference
nodes.description: Comprehensive guide for playwright-cli run-code: geolocation, permissions, media emulation, wait strategies, frames/iframes, file downloads, clipboard, page info, JS execution, error handling, complex workflows.

nodes.label: Session Management Reference
nodes.description: Named browser sessions, isolation properties, attach/detach (CDP, extension, channel), persistent profiles, configuration, concurrent scraping, A/B testing, best practices.

nodes.label: Spec-Driven Testing Reference
nodes.description: Three-phase workflow: planning (explore app, write spec file), generation (walk spec steps, emit TypeScript tests), healing (debug failures, fix locators, reconcile spec).

nodes.label: Storage State Reference
nodes.description: Complete storage management: save/restore full state, cookie CRUD with options, localStorage CRUD, sessionStorage CRUD, IndexedDB operations, authentication state reuse patterns.

nodes.label: Test Generation Reference
nodes.description: How playwright-cli actions generate Playwright TypeScript code. Semantic locators, assertion patterns (toHaveText, toBeVisible, toMatchAriaSnapshot), generate-locator command, best practices.

nodes.label: Tracing Reference
nodes.description: Execution trace capture with .trace (action log), .network (HTTP log), resources/ directory. Use cases: debugging failed actions, analyzing performance, capturing evidence. Comparison with video/screenshot.

nodes.label: Raw Output Mode (--raw)
nodes.description: Global flag stripping page status and snapshot sections from output, returning only the result value. Used for piping into other tools (jq, file redirection).

nodes.label: Tab Management
nodes.description: Commands for multi-tab workflows: tab-list, tab-new, tab-close, tab-select. Supports index-based tab switching.

nodes.label: Keyboard and Mouse Commands
nodes.description: Keyboard: press, keydown, keyup. Mouse: mousemove, mousedown, mouseup, mousewheel. Supports modifier keys and right-click.

nodes.label: Navigation Commands
nodes.description: go-back, go-forward, reload for browser history navigation.

nodes.label: Save As Commands
nodes.description: screenshot (page or element, with filename option) and pdf generation.

nodes.label: DevTools Commands
nodes.description: console (view console messages), requests (list network requests), request (inspect single request), run-code (advanced scripting), tracing, video, show --annotate (UI review), generate-locator, highlight.

nodes.label: playwright-cli Installation
nodes.description: Global install via npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest. Local fallback via npx --no-install playwright-cli.

nodes.label: Browser Configuration
nodes.description: Browser selection (chrome, firefox, webkit, msedge), persistent/in-memory profiles, CDP attach, Playwright Extension attach, config file support, headed mode.

nodes.label: Seed Test
nodes.description: Minimal Playwright test (navigation/login) that lands the page in the initial state for all spec scenarios. Foundation of the plan-generate-heal workflow.

nodes.label: Spec File
nodes.description: Markdown file (specs/<feature>.plan.md) containing application overview, grouped test scenarios with numbered steps and expect bullets. Each scenario maps to one test file.

nodes.label: Heal Workflow
nodes.description: Diagnose failing tests via debug mode, fix locator/assertion drift, reconcile spec with app behavior. Never use sleeps/networkidle; use test.fixme for confirmed bugs.

nodes.label: Playwright Video Recording
nodes.description: Capture browser automation sessions as video for debugging, documentation, or verification

nodes.label: WebM Format
nodes.description: Video output format using VP8/VP9 codec produced by Playwright screencast

nodes.label: Chapter Markers
nodes.description: Section transition markers in screencast recordings with description and duration

nodes.label: Overlay API
nodes.description: Custom HTML overlays for callouts, labels, highlights with pointer-events: none

nodes.label: Tracing vs Video
nodes.description: Comparison between video recording (WebM, demos) and tracing (DOM snapshots, debugging)

nodes.label: playwright-cli video-start
nodes.description: CLI command to start video recording with a specified output filename

nodes.label: playwright-cli video-chapter
nodes.description: CLI command to add a chapter marker during video recording

nodes.label: playwright-cli video-stop
nodes.description: CLI command to stop and save video recording

nodes.label: playwright-cli run-code
nodes.description: CLI command to execute a JavaScript script for automated hero scenarios

nodes.label: YARP Reverse Proxy
nodes.description: Yet Another Reverse Proxy used as API gateway for routing to backend services

nodes.label: Gateway Configuration
nodes.description: YARP gateway.yaml configuration defining routes, clusters, and destination mappings

nodes.label: Identity API Route
nodes.description: Route /api/User/{**catch-all} -> identity-cluster

nodes.label: Membership API Route
nodes.description: Route /api/members/{**catch-all} -> membership-cluster

nodes.label: Membership Plans Route
nodes.description: Route /api/plans/{**catch-all} -> membership-cluster

nodes.label: Identity OpenAPI Route
nodes.description: Route /docs/identity/{**catch-all} -> identity-cluster with path transform to /openapi/v1.json

nodes.label: Membership OpenAPI Route
nodes.description: Route /docs/membership/{**catch-all} -> membership-cluster with path transform to /openapi/v1.json

nodes.label: Identity Cluster
nodes.description: YARP cluster pointing to identity-api destination at http+https://identity-api

nodes.label: Membership Cluster
nodes.description: YARP cluster pointing to membership-api destination at http+https://membership-api

nodes.label: CORS Metadata
nodes.description: CORS policy applied to routes via metadata key cors: default

nodes.label: Path Transforms
nodes.description: YARP transform that rewrites request paths for OpenAPI documentation routes

nodes.label: FitTech Project Spec (chunk 1/2)
nodes.content:
Fiche de presentation du projet
Systeme de gestion d'une Salle de sport connectee « FitTech »
Titre complet
AMAR BENSABER Djamel
Encadreur
Resume Systeme de gestion d'une salle de sport connectee
Une salle de sport moderne, "FitTech", souhaite developper un systeme integre de
gestion des abonnements

nodes.label: FitTech Project Spec (chunk 2/2)
nodes.content:
en pause l'abonnement (maximum 2 mois par an).
- Reservation de cours :
  a. Les coachs creent des cours (spinning, crossfit, etc.) avec un nombre maximum de participants, date/heure, duree et niveau requis.
  b. Les membres peuvent reserver un cours via l'application mobile, jusqu'a 7 jours a l'ava
